title: Off-Hours Authentication (CIM)
spl:
index=security tag=authentication action=success
| eval hr=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hr<6 OR hr>22) AND (match(user,"^admin") OR match(user,"^svc_"))
| stats count by user, app, src
description: Privileged or bulk success auths outside business hours warrant review.
value: Privileged or bulk success auths outside business hours warrant review.
implementation: Join HR schedule lookup; reduce noise for global teams.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="success" by Authentication.user, Authentication.app, _time span=1h
| eval hr=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where hr<6 OR hr>22
knownFalsePositives: Failed and odd login patterns from rotated service accounts, monitoring tools with stale credentials, M&A user migrations, and scheduled batch jobs with expired secrets.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: CIM Authentication.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Normalized `_time` with user timezone optional.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Join HR schedule lookup; reduce noise for global teams.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=success
| eval hr=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hr<6 OR hr>22) AND (match(user,"^admin") OR match(user,"^svc_"))
| stats count by user, app, src
```

Understanding this SPL

**Off-Hours Authentication (CIM)** — Privileged or bulk success auths outside business hours warrant review.

Documented **Data sources**: Normalized `_time` with user timezone optional. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CIM Authentication. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hr** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where (hr<6 OR hr>22) AND (match(user,"^admin") OR match(user,"^svc_"))` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, app, src** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action="success" by Authentication.user, Authentication.app, _time span=1h
| eval hr=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where hr<6 OR hr>22
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Off-Hours Authentication (CIM)** — Privileged or bulk success auths outside business hours warrant review.

Documented **Data sources**: Normalized `_time` with user timezone optional. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CIM Authentication. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hr** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where hr<6 OR hr>22` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (off-hours auths), Heatmap (hour × day).
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Off-Hours Authentication" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Authentication.Authentication` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
